title: Lesson 38 — The Inventor

words.example: An ingenious solution used parts no one else considered.
The startup's innovative design won several awards.

words.definition: new and original; not seen before
words.example: She proposed a novel approach to an old problem.
The merger created a company of unprecedented size.

words.definition: contrary to what is usual, traditional, or accepted
words.example: His unorthodox training methods drew skeptical looks.

words.definition: new and experimental, especially in the arts
words.example: The gallery specialized in avant-garde installations.

words.definition: imitative of another's work; not original
words.example: Critics dismissed the sequel as derivative.

words.definition: so lacking in originality as to be obvious and boring
words.example: The dialogue felt banal, full of tired lines.

words.definition: based on generally accepted standards; ordinary
words.example: He took a conventional path through law school.

words.definition: conforming to established, accepted rules or beliefs
words.example: The paper stuck to orthodox economic theory.

words.definition: a first, typical, or preliminary model of something
words.example: The prototype worked, though the final version would look different.

words.definition: a person who is the first to do something; a pioneer
words.example: The engineer was a trailblazer in solar technology.

story: The design competition rewarded only one thing: an [[ingenious|ingenious]], truly [[innovative|innovative]] idea. Most entries offered a [[novel|novel]] twist on an old machine, but nothing felt [[unprecedented|unprecedented]]. One entrant went fully [[unorthodox|unorthodox]], almost [[avant-garde|avant-garde]], while several others submitted work so [[derivative|derivative]] and [[banal|banal]] the judges recognized it from last year. The safest, most [[conventional|conventional]] and [[orthodox|orthodox]] designs scored well but won no prizes. In the end the judges chose a rough [[prototype|prototype]] built by a first-time entrant, calling her a genuine [[trailblazer|trailblazer]].
